step_by_step_reasoning: Based on the transcript and the physical whiteboard configuration: 1. Salesforce (ThirdParty) triggers API Gateway (ApiGateway). 2. API Gateway directs requests to two Lambda functions: a Parser Lambda and a Routing Lambda. 3. Parser Lambda standardizes ad data and stores it in the Ad Data DynamoDB table. 4. Routing Lambda queries routing configurations and credentials from the Config DynamoDB table. 5. S3 hosts the front-end application used by Creative Designers (UserConsumerMobile). 6. Designers log in securely via Cognito user pools and run workflow processes through the Workflow Lambda function.

graph.name: Advanced Local: Automating Creative Ad Workflows
graph.notes: Advanced Local automated their ad creative workflow by integrating Salesforce triggers with AWS serverless services. Salesforce data is received by API Gateway and parsed/standardized by Lambda, then stored in a DynamoDB ad data table. A routing Lambda function reads configuration and credentials from a config DynamoDB table to route ads. Creative designers authenticate via Cognito, access a front-end app hosted on S3, read ad data from DynamoDB, and execute workflow logic via another Lambda function.

nodes.name: SalesForce
nodes.notes: Trigger source for completed product and creative data.

nodes.name: Config Table
nodes.notes: Stores routing configurations and downstream credentials.

nodes.name: Ad Data Table
nodes.notes: Stores parsed and standardized creative ad data.

nodes.name: Frontend App S3
nodes.notes: Hosts the front-end application for creative designers.

nodes.name: Parser Lambda
nodes.notes: Parses, standardizes, and formats Salesforce input before storing.

nodes.name: Routing Lambda
nodes.notes: Determines destination and credentials for ads based on Config Table.

nodes.name: Workflow Lambda
nodes.notes: Executes business logic for creative ad workflows.

nodes.name: Amazon Cognito
nodes.notes: Handles secure authentication and single sign-on (SSO) user pools.

nodes.name: Creative Designers
nodes.notes: Creative users who build and proof ads using the front-end app.

edges.notes: Triggers sent from Salesforce upon completion of product data.
Routes raw payload to Parser Lambda.
Stores parsed and standardized ad data.
Provides routing credentials and downstream configurations.
Hosts front-end app for the user.
